entry_point.controller: N/A — batch job (no HTTP entry point)

request_model.class: Warning (domain entity constructed in-process by batch service)
fields: WarningTypeId: int (enum WarningTypes: READyLeak, READyAmbientTemp, READyBurst)
DateCreatedUtc: DateTime (set to UtcNow by repository)
Recipients: List<WarningRecipient> ({ WarningId, Kvhx, StandardReceiverId, StandardReceiverGroupId, PhoneCode, PhoneNumber, Email })

sql_operations.operation: select count(*) FROM dbo.Warnings WHERE ProfileId=@ProfileId AND WarningTypeId=@WarningTypeId AND SourceRef=@SourceRef
sql_operations.note: Idempotency guard — skip if already exists

sql_operations.operation: BulkInsert Warning → then BulkInsert WarningRecipients + WarningFields (Z.Dapper.Plus, transacted)

validations: SourceRef uniqueness check before insert: WarningRepository.WarningExists() — skip duplicate if same profileId+warningTypeId+sourceRef (WarningService.cs:1)
ReadyService: only creates warning if sensor data meets threshold (leak/burst/ambientTemp candidate logic) (ReadyService.cs:300+)
side_effects: WarningWorkloadProcessor picks up new warnings (Status=unprocessed) and sends SMS/email via WarningMessageSender (WarningWorkloadProcessor.cs:1)
Batch job sets DateProcessedUtc and updates Status after sending (WarningService.ProcessWarningsAsync)
error_paths: Duplicate SourceRef → silently skipped (no error, no insert) (WarningService.cs:1)
Transaction rollback if BulkInsert fails — Warning + Fields + Recipients all-or-nothing (WarningRepository.cs:17)
notes: create_warning has NO HTTP entry point — it is exclusively triggered by internal batch jobs: ReadyService (FTP file processor for Kamstrup READy meters) and TrimbleOutageNotificationService (SOAP-based). WarningRecipient.ExternalReference field exists on entity but not mapped to WarningRecipients table in the BulkInsert — may be missing column. TrimbleOutageNotificationService entry: ServiceAlert.Web/Controllers/TrimbleOutageNotificationSoap.cs (SOAP endpoint) → TrimbleOutageNotificationService.CreateWarnings().
